sections.subtitle: Team Form
sections.text: Milan have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with impressive wins like 9-0 against Perth Glory but also a heavy 4-1 loss to Chelsea. Cremonese, on the other hand, have been more consistent defensively, with 3 clean sheets in their last 5 games, though their attacking output has been limited except in a 3-0 win over Aurora Pro Patria.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: The last two encounters between Milan and Cremonese ended in draws (1-1 and 0-0), indicating tightly contested matches. Milan dominated possession in both games but struggled to convert chances into goals, while Cremonese showed resilience defensively.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics and Streaks
sections.text: Milan, positioned 4th in the league, have a strong tendency to score first (4/5 matches) and maintain games with fewer than 4.5 cards. Cremonese, sitting 19th, have not conceded in their last 3 matches and consistently see under 10.5 corners in their games, suggesting a low-scoring, defensive setup.

sections.subtitle: Match Factors
sections.text: Played at San Siro, Milan are likely to leverage home advantage and their superior attacking stats. Cremonese's recent defensive form and the referee's average of 4.8 yellow cards per game suggest a cautious, physical game with limited goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at San Siro saw an intriguing battle between Milan and Cremonese, with the scoreboard reading 0-1 in favor of the visitors at the break. Cremonese managed to take the lead through a well-executed header, capitalizing on one of their limited chances. Milan, despite dominating possession and creating more entries into the final third, struggled to convert their opportunities into goals. The home side hit the woodwork and showed intent, but their finishing let them down. Cremonese, on the other hand, were clinical with their limited possession, defending stoutly and making the most of their moment in the penalty area.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Milan started the game with a strong attacking presence, maintaining consistent pressure in the early stages with several threatening spells in the opposition half. Their momentum was evident as they pushed forward repeatedly, controlling the tempo for long stretches. However, Cremonese shifted the dynamics around the midway point of the half, building a crucial attacking spell that led to their opening goal. After conceding, Milan attempted to regain their earlier rhythm with bursts of forward play, especially towards the end of the half, but couldn't break through. Cremonese managed to weather these surges, holding firm with brief but effective counter-attacking moments.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key team statistics
halftimeSections.text: Milan's dominance in possession was clear, as they controlled the ball for the majority of the half and registered significantly more entries into the final third and penalty area compared to Cremonese. Their expected goals also reflected a higher likelihood of scoring, though they couldn't capitalize on big chances created. Cremonese, despite being on the back foot for much of the half, showed efficiency with their shots on target, converting one of their few clear opportunities. Defensively, they were solid, winning a higher percentage of duels and maintaining a balanced aerial presence alongside Milan.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Standout performers
halftimeSections.text: For Milan, Strahinja Pavlović made an impact with a goal just before the break, showing his aerial prowess and defensive stability. Pervis Estupiñán also stood out with a key assist and creative contributions in attack. On the Cremonese side, Federico Baschirotto was exceptional, scoring the opener with a powerful header and anchoring the defense with multiple clearances and tackles. Alessio Zerbin also contributed significantly with an assist and strong defensive work rate, helping Cremonese maintain their lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Milan's approach relied heavily on building play through the final third, aiming to overload Cremonese's defensive structure with width and quick passing. However, their lack of clinical finishing prevented them from leveling the score. Cremonese adopted a more pragmatic style, sitting deep to absorb pressure and looking to exploit transitional moments, which paid off with their goal. Their compact defensive shape frustrated Milan's attacking efforts, forcing the home side into speculative shots or hurried decisions in the final third.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Heading into the second half, Milan are likely to intensify their attacking efforts, pushing for an equalizer with increased urgency. They will need to be more precise in front of goal and may look to introduce fresh legs to break down Cremonese's defensive wall. Cremonese will aim to protect their slender lead, potentially focusing on time-wasting tactics and counter-attacks to catch Milan off-guard. If Milan can maintain their possession dominance and sharpen their finishing, they stand a good chance of turning the game around. However, Cremonese's resilience could see them hold firm or even nick another goal on the break.
